race.name: Tour of Georgia Gran Fondo
race.tagline: Challenging timed-segment Gran Fondo through North Georgia Mountains

climate.description: Spring in North Georgia Mountains; mild temperatures with potential for variable weather including rain.

features: Steep climbs, quiet country roads, scenic views, thrilling descents through National Forest lands and mountain gaps

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 77. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 71.

course_description.character: Crisscrosses Appalachian Trail through North Georgia Mountains: steep climbs, secluded roads, National Forest, mountain gaps.
course_description.signature_challenge: 9,000ft Gran with 4 timed climbs over brutal gaps

logistics.lodging_strategy: Hotels in Bavarian-themed Helen; book early for event weekend.
logistics.transport: Staged mass start from Helen parking lot; mandatory bib/plate/chip; 10mph progression cutoff; app for results/GPS.

history.origin_story: Part of Gran Fondo National Series (est. 2012); Stop 2; no specific event founding year found.
notable_moments: Picks for best Southeast road events
Custom Suarez kits for Gran age winners

biased_opinion.summary: A gravel god's road detour: savage climbs and series polish make the Gran a bucket-list sufferfest, even without dirt.

final_verdict.one_liner: Elite climbing gran fondo in alpine Georgia—pure vertical pain with Bavarian charm.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Top-tier GFNS event with massive elevation, timed KOMs, full support, and series perks. Gran route crushes souls over big gaps; perfect for roadies chasing suffering and swag.

Footage from the 2023 Tour of Georgia. Gran Fondo National Series.

videos.title: Tour of Georgia Gran Fondo 2024 / Shimano
videos.description:
Our series partner @shimanoroad did a tremendous job supporting all the riders in very challenging conditions, with Moto keeping an eye on all the riders out on course at all times to make sure everyone made it back to the finish line to celebrate/commiserate. Check out the highlights from the Tour of Georgia Gran Fondo in Helen, Georgia! 🚴‍♂️☔️ #ShimanoSupport #CyclingCommunity 

Our next event is Highlands Gran Fondo on June 2nd in Butler New Jersey. Sign up here: 
https://www.granfondonationalseries.com/gran-fondo-highlands

videos.title: TT1 Tour of Georgia Gran Fondo Highlights 2021
videos.description:
https://www.granfondonationalseries.com/gran-fondo-georgia

The 2021 Colnago Gran Fondo National Series continued with the TT1 Tour of Georgia Gran Fondo on April 18 in Helen, GA. Beautiful weather and a scenic, challenging course on quiet mountain roads led to 120% growth in participation over 2019! With three courses, wave starts, staffed and socially distanced aid stations, and timed-segment competitions, riders had a great time competing, regrouping to ride with friends, and enjoying the views. 

Results: https://www.granfondonationalseries.com/2021

videos.title: Gran Fondo National Series : Brutal Racing in Georgia
videos.description:
I thought Gran Fondos weren’t supposed to be races… then I showed up to this one.

Welcome to Helen, Georgia — home of one of the toughest (and most beautiful) Gran Fondo National Series events in the USA. 90 miles. 9,000 feet of climbing. And oh yeah… four timed stages that turn this “casual ride” into a full-blown sufferfest.

In this video, I take you through the full experience — the climbs, the chaos, and whether all that winter training actually paid off. Plus, we’ll answer the big question:
Is this the best Gran Fondo in the USA?

Hit subscribe if you're into cycling, pain, or watching someone question their life choices on a climb.

videos.curation_reason: Recent first-person race experience with detailed course breakdown and specific terrain descriptions.

videos.title: FULL GAS From Start to Finish 😤 | GF Georgia Helen | 14th of 120
videos.description:
This race was part of the SENA Gran Fondo National Series E-Fondo Series on ROUVY, and unlike some of the other events where strategy and recovery between segments matter… this one was simply full gas from start to finish.

GF Georgia – Route 1 (Helen, USA) is relentless — rolling terrain with a hard climb segment that never really lets you settle. There was no hiding, no chill sections, just steady pressure the entire race.

I finished 14th overall out of 120 riders, holding consistent power the whole way and staying committed even when the legs started to fade.

⸻

📊 Race Stats
 • Avg Power: 208 W
 • Normalized Power: 215 W
 • Max Power: 356 W
 • 5s: 324 W
 • 30s: 284 W
 • 1m: 261 W
 • 5m: 244 W
 • 20m: 230 W
 • 30m: 228 W
 • 60m: 213 W

⸻

💭 Race Takeaway

This was a fitness test, not a tactical one.
Sustained effort, holding pace, and surviving the climb made the difference. These are the races that quietly build the engine.

⸻


#ROUVY #EFondo #GranFondo #GFCheaha #GFGerogia #HelenGeorgia #VirtualCycling #IndoorCycling #WomenWhoRace #CyclingRace #ClimbingRide #PainCave #EnduranceCycling #cyclinglife

quotes.text: 90 miles with 9,000 ft of climbing. The first stage was like 3 mi from the start, which meant this nice little climb was going to be our warm up. And by little, I mean 7.2 mi and 1,500 ft of elevation.

We're rolling out of Helen, Georgia. This is a small mountain town that looks like someone Googled Germany and hit copy and paste. It's like the most random place I've ever seen, but I kind of love it.

This one is about 1,700 ft of climbing. I always forget how flat the beginning section is and just how steep it gets at the end. Every time I've done this climb, I will tell myself halfway that I should really learn how gear ratios work.

key_challenges.name: First Stage Climb
key_challenges.description: 7.2 mile climb with 1,500 ft of elevation gain starting just 3 miles from the start. Rider described it as brutal, taking just under 30 minutes to complete.

key_challenges.name: Second Stage Climb
key_challenges.description: 1,700 ft of climbing that starts flat and gets very steep at the end. Riders consistently forget how flat the beginning is and how steep it becomes, making pacing difficult.

key_challenges.name: Stage Three Positioning
key_challenges.description: Just under 4 miles with only 400 ft of elevation, but positioning at the start is crucial. Being on the right side of the group is problematic - need to get to the front quickly.

race_day_tips.text: Position yourself close to the front before flat stages, not on the right side of large groups where you can get stuck.
Find a group for drafting, especially on flatter sections - riding solo will kill your time.
Remember that the second stage climb starts flat and gets steep at the end - pace accordingly instead of going hard early.

additional_quotes.text: Helen, Georgia is a small mountain town that looks like someone Googled Germany and hit copy and paste. It's like the most random place I've ever seen, but I kind of love it.

This one's different. It's actually a race, but only kind of. There will be four time stages throughout the course. That's what we'll actually be racing. The rest, you can ride that as slow as you want.

rider_intel.search_text: The Tour of Georgia Gran Fondo features four timed stages with non-competitive sections between them, totaling about 90 miles and 9,000 feet of climbing. The race starts in Helen, Georgia, a German-themed mountain town. The first major challenge comes at mile 3 with a 7.2-mile, 1,500-foot climb that takes most riders around 30 minutes. The second stage features 1,700 feet of climbing that deceives riders by starting flat before becoming very steep. Riders consistently struggle with pacing this section. The third stage is flatter at just under 4 miles with 400 feet of elevation, but positioning at the start is critical for staying with groups. Drafting is essential on flatter sections, and being stuck without a group significantly impacts times. The competitive riders are described as mountain goats, with significant time gaps between ability levels.
